reproducible_artifact: Contract test suite + deprecation calendar policy + PR review label
embodiment_sketch: Three concrete artifacts: (1) a contract-test suite (Pact, schemathesis, or hand-rolled) that exercises the canonical wire shape (POST /oauth/token, request schema, response schema, status codes, header semantics) and runs in CI; (2) a deprecation-calendar policy document naming required notice windows, migration-guide requirements, and dual-write phases; (3) a PR label `contract-affecting` that requires explicit approval from the API governance group. Each artifact embodies a distinct claim of the verse: contract tests = 'the imperishable is testable'; deprecation calendar = 'no single team's authority can destroy it without process'; PR label = 'the substrate is named in the workflow.'
verdict_reason: The verse's claim ('the protocol persists; nobody on the team can destroy it without process') reduces to three implementable artifacts. Not pure metaphor. PASS.
